title: Sign In Page (`app/sign-in/[[...sign-in]]/page.tsx`)
content:
This file defines the sign-in page for the DocMed application, leveraging Clerk for authentication and custom UI components for an animated and visually engaging experience. ## Purpose The primary purpose of this page is to provide users with a secure and user-friendly interface to sign into their DocMed accounts. It supports email/password authentication and social login via Google. ## Key Components and Logic * **`useSignIn` (Clerk):** Manages the sign-in process, including creating sessions and handling authentication strategies. * **`useRouter` (Next.js):** Used for programmatic navigation after successful authentication. * **`useState`:** Manages the local state for email, password, and error messages. * **`AnimatedForm`:** A custom UI component from `@/components/ui/modern-animated-sign-in` that renders the sign-in form with animations and interactive elements. * **`Ripple` and `TechOrbitDisplay`:** Custom UI components from `@/components/ui/modern-animated-sign-in` that provide background visual effects for the sign-in page. * **`OrbitIcon` Component:** A helper component to render icons within the orbit display, styled with specific sizes and colors. * **`orbitIcons` Array:** Configuration for the `TechOrbitDisplay` component, defining the icons, their animations, and placement. * **`handleSubmit`:** An asynchronous function that handles the form submission for email/password sign-in. It calls Clerk's `signIn.create` method and updates the session upon successful completion. * **`handleGoogleLogin`:** An asynchronous function that initiates the Google OAuth flow using Clerk's `signIn.authenticateWithRedirect`. * **`formFields` Object:** A configuration object passed to the `AnimatedForm` component, defining labels, types, placeholders, and event handlers for the form fields. ## Usage Example This component is rendered directly as a page at the `/sign-in` route. It automatically handles the Clerk authentication flow. ```typescript // app/sign-in/[[...sign-in]]/page.tsx // ... imports and helper components ... export default function SignInPage() { // ... state and hooks ... return (
{/* Left — orbit display */}
{/* Right — form */}
{}} errorField={error} />

Don't have an account?{' '} Sign up free

); } ``` This page dynamically handles the sign-in process, redirecting users to `/dashboard` upon successful authentication or displaying an error message if sign-in fails.
